tasks.title: Skip auth check for internal callback requests
tasks.description: This PR simplifies callback handling for our payment webhook endpoint.
tasks.diff:
diff --git a/payments/views.py b/payments/views.py
@@
-def handle_webhook(request):
-    if not verify_signature(request):
-        return HttpResponseForbidden()
+def handle_webhook(request):
+    internal = request.headers.get('X-Internal-Call') == 'true'
+    if not internal and not verify_signature(request):
+        return HttpResponseForbidden()
     payload = json.loads(request.body)
     process_payment_event(payload)
     return HttpResponse(status=204)

diff --git a/payments/tests/test_webhooks.py b/payments/tests/test_webhooks.py
@@
 def test_invalid_signature_is_rejected(client):
     response = client.post('/webhook', data='{}', content_type='application/json')
     assert response.status_code == 403
+
+def test_internal_calls_skip_signature(client):
+    response = client.post('/webhook', data='{}', content_type='application/json', HTTP_X_INTERNAL_CALL='true')
+    assert response.status_code == 204
required_findings: header based auth bypass allows spoofing requests
webhook endpoint should always verify signature for untrusted traffic
tests only cover happy path and miss malicious forged header case
nice_to_have_findings: internal service authentication should use signed token or network boundary
merge_blockers: A client can forge X-Internal-Call and bypass signature validation.
The new test normalizes insecure behavior instead of guarding against abuse.

tasks.title: Optimize save handler in settings panel
tasks.description: Refactors the save handler to reduce rerenders in the settings form.
required_findings: snapshot captures stale settings from an earlier render
save handler may send outdated values after user edits
no test covers changing settings before clicking save
nice_to_have_findings: use latest state inside handler instead of render snapshot
merge_blockers: The save call can persist stale form state.
The PR claims optimization but introduces correctness risk.

tasks.title: Update README with new installation instructions
tasks.description: Adds clear installation steps for the new Docker setup.
tasks.diff:
diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
@@
 ## Installation
 
-Clone the repository and run `npm install`.
+Clone the repository and run `npm install`.
+
+### Docker Setup (Recommended)
+
+1. Ensure Docker is installed on your system
+2. Run `docker build -t myapp .`
+3. Run `docker run -p 3000:3000 myapp`
+4. Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser
required_findings: no breaking changes to existing content
